definitions.text: done carefully and with a lot of attention to detail
examples: a rigorous analysis
Few people have gone into the topic in such rigorous detail.
The second team adopted a much more rigorous approach to the problem.

definitions.text: demanding that particular rules, processes, etc. are strictly followed
examples: The work failed to meet their rigorous standards .
They may benefit from the rigorous application of competition policy.

definitions.text: the total amount of crops, profits, etc. that are produced
examples: a high crop yield
a reduction in milk yield
This will give a yield of 10% on your investment.

definitions.text: a total number or amount made up of smaller amounts that are collected together
examples: a record aggregate of 285 points
The three smaller parties gained an aggregate of 25 per cent of the vote.

definitions.text: sand or broken stone that is used to make concrete or for building roads, etc.
added together as a total or single amount
examples: Businesses are, in the aggregate, deeper in debt than ever before.

definitions.text: when the scores of a number of games are added together
examples: They won 4–2 on aggregate.

definitions.text: physically or mentally ill
examples: a sick child
a sick relative/patient
Her mother's very sick.
Peter has been off sick (= away from work because he is ill) for two weeks.
Emma has just called in sick (= phoned to say she will not be coming to work because she is ill) .
Britain's workers went sick (= did not go to work because they were ill) for a record number of days last year.
I can't afford to get sick (= become ill) .
They became sick after eating contaminated food.
I have been sick in bed since you left.
She was sick with the flu.
Many people were sick from diseases caused by pollution.

definitions.text: feeling that you want to vomit
examples: Mum, I feel sick !
If you eat any more cake you'll make yourself sick .
a sick feeling in your stomach

definitions.text: feeling sick as a result of travelling on a ship, plane, etc.
examples: seasick
airsick
carsick
travel-sick

definitions.text: bored with or annoyed about something that has been happening for a long time, and wanting it to stop
examples: I'm sick of the way you've treated me.
I'm sick and tired of your moaning.
I'm sick to death of all of you!
We are getting heartily sick of your attitude.
We're sick of waiting around like this.
I am sick of hearing about them and their wedding.
I'm so sick of him whining about how hard his job is.

definitions.text: dealing with physical or mental pain, disease or death in a cruel way that some people think is offensive
examples: a sick joke
That's really sick.

definitions.text: getting pleasure from doing strange or cruel things
examples: a sick mind
People think I'm sick for having a rat as a pet.
We live in a sick society.

definitions.text: very good, a lot of fun, etc.
examples: I love that song—it’s sick!

definitions.text: to bring food from your stomach back out through your mouth
examples: I was sick three times in the night.
She had been violently sick .

definitions.text: to be extremely worried
examples: Where have you been? I've been worried sick about you.
She was worrying herself sick about the exams.

definitions.text: to become sick
examples: A week later he fell sick and died.
He fell sick with yellow fever.

definitions.text: to make somebody angry or full of horror
examples: His hypocrisy makes me sick.
Her attitude makes me sick.

definitions.text: feeling very sick; vomiting a lot
very disappointed
very unhappy or disappointed
feeling very angry or worried
examples: Nora turned sick to her stomach on hearing this news.
It makes me sick to my stomach to hear such stupid attitudes.

definitions.text: feeling that you want to vomit
a typical example or pattern of something
examples: a paradigm for students to copy
The war was a paradigm of the destructive side of human nature.

definitions.text: a set of all the different forms of a word
examples: verb paradigms
